source: USFS Urban FIA active inventory projects (2024)
discovered_via: Texas A&M 'My City's Trees' (mct.tfs.tamu.edu) is a Nuxt SPA frontend; the actual data is from the federal Urban FIA program. Texas A&M's stats page (texasforestinfo.tamu.edu/urbanforeststats) loads the program boundaries from an openly-readable Esri FeatureServer — the source_url above.
rationale: Cities participating in the federal Urban FIA program nearly always have their own municipal point-level tree inventory. The FIA data itself is plot-sample statistical and not point-level; we don't import it. But the city list is a strong discovery seed — for each city, look on its own AGOL org / open-data portal for a separate FeatureServer with point geometry.

tracked_unknown_status.name: San Antonio, TX
tracked_unknown_status.known_state: tracker entry says license_status=gated, TreesSA FeatureServer requires token

investigation_workflow_per_city: 1. ArcGIS Online search: owner:CityOf<X> OR 'City of <X>' trees
2. Verify a FeatureServer with point geometry + species field
3. If found and clean license: write scripts/import/<slug>-trees.ts
4. If gated (token required, Cloudflare-protected, login-required): flag as Phase-3 outreach
